intro: Verhuellia hydrocotylifolia, also known as Indian pennywort or Gotu Kola, is a perennial herb native to tropical Asia, belonging to the family Araliaceae. This plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation and anxiety, improving cognitive function and memory, and enhancing wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Verhuellia hydrocotylifolia include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and polyphenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include capsules, tablets, and teas, as well as topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
